purpose: Classify all remaining production-capable bypass paths after issue #584 containment measures. Residual bypasses are root-authority surfaces that cannot be fully eliminated from repository code alone.

classification_key.P1: Mutation capability with governance controls in place
classification_key.P2: Mutation capability with partial governance controls; requires external action
classification_key.P3: Root authority bypass; requires account-level credentials

residual_bypasses.name: Cloudflare Git Integration (not yet disabled)
preconditions: Cloudflare Git Integration enabled at account level
residual_bypasses.observability: Cloudflare dashboard deploy log only; not bound to MindShift proof registry
residual_bypasses.closure_condition: Git Integration disabled at Cloudflare account level and verified by administrator

residual_bypasses.name: Direct wrangler deploy with valid CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN
residual_bypasses.observability: Cloudflare dashboard deploy log only; not bound to MindShift proof registry
residual_bypasses.closure_condition: CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN scoped to GitHub Actions OIDC only; no static token capable of production deploy

residual_bypasses.name: Local governed-deploy.ts with fabricated artifact and valid token
preconditions: Fabricated legitimacy artifact with valid canonical chain responses
residual_bypasses.observability: AUDIT_LOG — deploy_audit_registry.json entry emitted (rejection or success); Cloudflare dashboard deploy log
residual_bypasses.status: RESIDUAL — requires canonical chain artifact (must hit live /session→/proof endpoints) AND token AND context
residual_bypasses.closure_condition: CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN scoped to GitHub Actions OIDC only

residual_bypasses.name: GitHub Actions admin workflow modification
preconditions: Ability to bypass branch protection or merge without PREO
residual_bypasses.observability: GitHub audit log; constitutional-integrity.yml detects workflow changes as PR-3_SOVEREIGNTY drift
residual_bypasses.status: CLASSIFIED_OPEN — requires admin credentials; monitored by constitutional-integrity.yml
residual_bypasses.closure_condition: Branch protection enforcement at target level; admin bypass disabled

residual_bypasses.name: GitHub repository secrets mutation (MINDSHIFT_WORKER_URL redirect)
preconditions: GitHub repository admin or environments admin access
Ability to mutate MINDSHIFT_WORKER_URL or MINDSHIFT_API_KEY secrets
residual_bypasses.observability: GitHub audit log only; MindShift has no visibility into secret mutation
residual_bypasses.closure_condition: Secret write authority restricted to designated administrators; secret rotation policy enforced

bypass_closure_requirements: RB-001: Disable Cloudflare Git Integration at account level
RB-002: Scope CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN to GitHub Actions OIDC
RB-003: Resolved by RB-002 closure (no local token = no local deploy)
RB-004: Enforce branch protection rules and admin bypass policy per BRANCH_PROTECTION_POLICY.json
RB-005: Restrict secret write access per organization policy
acceptance_condition: No production-capable Cloudflare mutation path exists outside governed deployment lineage. Currently blocked by: RB-001 (requires account action). All other residual bypasses are classified root-authority surfaces observable in Cloudflare dashboard and/or GitHub audit log.
observability_requirement: All production deploy events — governed or bypass — MUST be detectable. RB-001 and RB-002 are only visible in Cloudflare dashboard (external observability). RB-003 is visible in deploy_audit_registry.json. RB-004 and RB-005 visible in GitHub audit log. No production mutation path is fully unobservable.
